title: betula affinis
intro: Betula affinis, also known as Himalayan birch, is a deciduous tree species native to the Himalayan Mountains. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Betula affinis include relief from fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as support for respiratory and cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Betula affinis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
